9: Gemini CLI is equipped with a suite of built-in tools that allow it to interact with its
10: environment. These include tools for file system operations (like reading and writing), a
11: shell tool for running commands, and tools for accessing the internet via web fetching
12: and searching. For broader context, it uses specialized tools to read multiple files at
13: once and a memory tool to save information for later sessions. This functionality is built

18: Aider is an open-source AI coding assistant that acts as a true pair programmer by
19: working directly on your files and committing changes to Git. Its defining feature is its
20: directness; it applies edits, runs tests to validate them, and automatically commits every
21: successful change. Being model-agnostic, it gives users complete control over cost and
22: capabilities. Its git-centric workflow makes it perfect for developers who value efficiency,
23: control, and a transparent, auditable trail of all code modifications.
25: 1. Test-Driven Development (TDD): A developer can say: "Create a failing test for
26: a function that calculates the factorial of a number." After Aider writes the test
27: and it fails, the next prompt is: "Now, write the code to make the test pass." Aider
28: implements the function and runs the test again to confirm.
29: 2. Precise Bug Squashing: Given a bug report, you can instruct Aider: "The
30: calculate_total function in billing.py fails on leap years. Add the file to the context,
31: fix the bug, and verify your fix against the existing test suite."
32: 3. Dependency Updates: You could instruct it: "Our project uses an outdated
33: version of the 'requests' library. Please go through all Python files, update the
34: import statements and any deprecated function calls to be compatible with the
35: latest version, and then update requirements.txt."
